metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1322
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Virmani et al focus on the differential role of synptotagmin-7 (syt-7) splicing variants on synaptic vesicle endocytosis. The syt-7 gene undergoes extensive alternative splicing. Here the authors function on two of these variants: syt-7 A, the full-length variant containing C2A and C2B domains a the C-terminal end of the protein, and syt-7 B, a short variant resulting from an in-frame stop codon in a conserved exon, resulting in a protein lacking the two C2 domains.
Using unloading and subsequent uptake of FM-dyes to monitor endocytosis, the data in Fig. 2-4 show that expression of the two variants has opposite effects on endocytosis of synaptic vesicles: syt-7 B accelarates but syt-7A retards endocytosis (Fig. 5 and 7).

To this end, the authors express each splicing variant as CFP fusion protein (attached to the C-terminus) and induce exocytosis of the readily releasable pool by short, intense stimulation (30 Hz, 2s) in the presence of FM1-43/FM2-10 dye in order to label actively recycling SVs. Then, various washing and reloading protocols are applied in order to assess the average size and heterogeneity of the recycling SV pools at these synapses. The data show that syt-7B expression increases the size of the SV pool labeled during the short stimulation, and the change in the slope (Fig. 2 D-E) indicates increase in the heterogeneity of the vesicle pool (findings abolished by a double C->A mutation in a mutant syt-7B form, marked as syt-7B*). However, the size of the total recycling pool of vesicles is not altered. The authors show that this observation is not due to a changed relative size of RRP (Fig. 3), but rather due to increase of the endocytosis rate (Fig. 4) as shown by comparison of F1/F2 for control and syt-7B expressing neurons with or without dye washing delay (abolished increase in FM labeling upon delayed washing post-stimulation).
In contrast, expression of syt-7A causes a shift in the size of FM labeled SVs only during maximal stimulation (Fig. 5). Rather than changing the initial release of vesicles, overexpresssion of syt-7A causes faster synaptic depression during 10 Hz stimulation (Fig. 7) consistent with the delayed recycling of exocytosed vesicles, as observed also on EM, Fig. 6 (to be noted that other parameters such as RRP size, kinetics of release etc remain unchanged in control vs syt-7A expression neurons). From these observations, the authors conclude that syt-7A expression causes SV to enter a slow endocytotic recycling pathway.. Experimental description: Materials and Methods (Cell culture):
Dissociated hippocampal cultures were prepared from 1- to 2-day-old Sprague Dawley rats

1/2/2018 Pim
- Species of overexpressed Synaptotagmin-7 A/B isoforms is rat; traced back to PMID:11395007: "The alternatively spliced sequences were not only observed in PCR experiments with single-stranded cDNA from rat brain, but also in cDNA clones isolated from a rat brain cDNA library..."
